bead.title: bead review: reuse Vale-backed prose findings
bead.description:
PROBLEM
Bead review should be able to use normalized Vale-backed prose findings without confusing prose advice with correctness review.

ROOT CAUSE
docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-05-06-prose-quality-integration.md:264-283 requires prose findings and correctness review to stay separate. The plan also lists `ddx bead review --prose` reuse after the command engine lands at lines 356-359.

SMART JUSTIFICATION: Review integration is policy-sensitive. A weak implementation could turn advisory prose checks into blocking correctness gates or mix style findings into acceptance-criterion verdicts.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add or update the `ddx bead review --prose` path to reuse normalized prose findings.
- Attach prose evidence separately from acceptance/correctness review evidence.
- Keep prose findings advisory by default.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not make prose findings block bead closure.
- Do not run prose checks for non-doc changes.
- Do not alter reviewer model routing.

DEPS
Depends on the Vale-backed public `ddx doc prose` command.
bead.acceptance:
1. `TestBeadReviewProse_UsesNormalizedDocProseFindings` covers reuse of the Vale-backed findings.
2. `TestBeadReviewProse_EvidenceSeparateFromCorrectnessReview` covers evidence separation.
3. `TestBeadReviewProse_AdvisoryByDefault` proves findings do not block closure.
4. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... green.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
